---
title: "Week 1-2: Web Page Designing & HTML Fundamentals"
subtitle: "Topics: Web Development Strategies, Internet History, HTTP Protocols, HTML Structure & Basic Tags"
---

## Unit Overview

This unit introduces you to the fundamentals of web development, covering the history and evolution of the web, the protocols that power modern internet communication, and the essential HTML markup language that forms the foundation of every web page.

**Course Outcome:** CO-1 (K2 - Understanding)  
**Bloom's Level:** L3  
**Duration:** 2 weeks  
**Credits:** 3 hours theory

---

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this unit, you will be able to:

- Explain the evolution of the Internet and World Wide Web
- Understand HTTP/HTTPS protocols and client-server communication
- Create well-structured HTML5 documents
- Use semantic HTML elements appropriately
- Apply best practices in web page markup

---

## {{< fa play-circle >}} Lecture Slides & Videos

### Slide Deck 1: Internet & Web Fundamentals
- **Duration:** 45 minutes
- **Topics Covered:** 
  - What is the Internet?
  - Evolution of the Web
  - Client-Server Architecture
  - How Browsers Work

{{< fa file-pdf >}} [Download Slides (PDF)](coming-soon.pdf) | {{< fa video >}} [Watch Lecture Video](coming-soon)

### Slide Deck 2: HTTP Protocols Deep Dive
- **Duration:** 40 minutes  
- **Topics Covered:**
  - HTTP Request-Response Model
  - HTTP Methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
  - Status Codes
  - HTTPS and Security

{{< fa file-pdf >}} [Download Slides (PDF)](coming-soon.pdf) | {{< fa video >}} [Watch Lecture Video](coming-soon)

### Slide Deck 3: HTML5 Basics & Structure
- **Duration:** 50 minutes
- **Topics Covered:**
  - What is HTML?
  - HTML5 Document Structure
  - Semantic vs Non-Semantic Elements
  - Accessibility Best Practices

{{< fa file-pdf >}} [Download Slides (PDF)](coming-soon.pdf) | {{< fa video >}} [Watch Lecture Video](coming-soon)

---

## Class 1: Introduction to Web Page Designing & Web Development Strategies

### {{< fa book-open >}} Lecture Notes

#### What is Web Page Designing?

Web page designing is the art and science of creating functional, visually appealing, and user-friendly web pages. It involves:

- **Content Strategy:** Planning what information to present
- **Visual Design:** Creating appealing layouts and styling
- **User Experience (UX):** Ensuring ease of use and intuitive navigation
- **Technical Implementation:** Writing clean, semantic code
- **Performance:** Optimizing for speed and accessibility

#### Web Development Lifecycle

```
Phase 1: Planning & Analysis
├─ Understand requirements
├─ Define user personas
├─ Plan information architecture
└─ Set objectives

Phase 2: Design & Wireframing
├─ Create wireframes
├─ Design mockups
├─ Plan user flows
└─ Get stakeholder approval

Phase 3: Development & Coding
├─ Write HTML structure
├─ Apply CSS styling
├─ Add JavaScript interactivity
└─ Implement backend logic

Phase 4: Testing & QA
├─ Browser compatibility testing
├─ Performance testing
├─ Security audits
└─ Accessibility testing

Phase 5: Deployment & Launch
├─ Set up hosting
├─ Configure domain
├─ Final checks
└─ Go live!

Phase 6: Maintenance & Updates
├─ Monitor performance
├─ Fix bugs
├─ Update content
└─ Add new features
```

### {{< fa lightbulb >}} Key Concepts

**Responsive Design:**
- Creates layouts that work on all devices
- Uses flexible grids and media queries
- Provides optimal viewing experience

**Progressive Enhancement:**
- Start with basic HTML functionality
- Add CSS for presentation
- Layer JavaScript for interactivity

**Accessibility First:**
- Design for everyone including people with disabilities
- Use semantic HTML
- Provide alternative text and descriptions

### {{< fa code >}} Code Example

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Professional Web Page</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Your content -->
</body>
</html>
```

---

## Class 2: History of Internet & Web, HTTP Protocols

### {{< fa book-open >}} Lecture Notes

#### Timeline: From ARPANET to Modern Web

| Year | Event | Significance |
|------|-------|--------------|
| **1969** | ARPANET created | Foundation of modern internet |
| **1974** | TCP/IP protocols developed | Standardized internet communication |
| **1983** | DNS system introduced | Human-readable domain names |
| **1989** | Tim Berners-Lee proposes Web | Birth of World Wide Web |
| **1991** | First website goes live | `info.cern.ch` |
| **1993** | Mosaic browser released | Made web accessible to public |
| **1995** | HTTP/1.0 standardized | Web becomes commercial |
| **1995-2005** | Web 1.0 / Dot-com Era | Early web, static sites |
| **2004** | Web 2.0 begins | User-generated content, social media |
| **2009** | HTML5 development starts | Modern semantic markup |
| **2014** | HTML5 becomes standard | Official W3C recommendation |
| **2020s** | Modern Web | PWAs, SPA frameworks, Edge computing |

#### The Client-Server Model

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                INTERNET                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ↓                           ↓
    ┌─────────┐                  ┌────────┐
    │  CLIENT │ ←→ HTTP Request  │ SERVER │
    │(Browser)│ ←→ HTTP Response │ (Web)  │
    └─────────┘                  └────────┘
    • Display                    • Process
    • Interact                   • Store
    • Request                    • Respond
```

#### HTTP Protocol Explained

**HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol)** is stateless, request-response protocol.

**Request Example:**
```http
GET /index.html HTTP/1.1
Host: www.example.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0
Accept: text/html
Connection: keep-alive
```

**Response Example:**
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8
Content-Length: 5239
Server: Apache/2.4
Date: Mon, 10 Feb 2025 10:30:00 GMT

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<!-- HTML content -->
</html>
```

#### HTTP Status Codes

**2xx Success:**
- `200 OK` - Request successful
- `201 Created` - Resource created
- `204 No Content` - Success, no content

**3xx Redirection:**
- `301 Moved Permanently` - Page moved
- `302 Found` - Temporary redirect
- `304 Not Modified` - Use cached version

**4xx Client Error:**
- `400 Bad Request` - Invalid request
- `401 Unauthorized` - Authentication required
- `403 Forbidden` - Access denied
- `404 Not Found` - Resource doesn't exist

**5xx Server Error:**
- `500 Internal Server Error` - Server failure
- `502 Bad Gateway` - Invalid gateway response
- `503 Service Unavailable` - Server overloaded

#### HTTPS: Secure Communication

HTTPS encrypts data using SSL/TLS protocols, essential for:
- Protecting passwords
- Securing financial transactions
- Maintaining user privacy
- Building trust with users

---

## Class 3: HTML Introduction & Basic Structure

### {{< fa book-open >}} Lecture Notes

#### What is HTML?

**HTML (HyperText Markup Language)** describes the structure and content of web pages using semantic markup elements and tags.

```html
<!-- Element structure -->
<tagname attribute="value">Content</tagname>
```

#### Essential HTML5 Structure

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
    
<head>
    <!-- Metadata and document configuration -->
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <meta name="description" content="Page description">
    <title>Page Title</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">
</head>

<body>
    <!-- Visible content -->
    <header>
        <nav>Navigation menu</nav>
    </header>
    
    <main>
        <h1>Main heading</h1>
        <p>Content paragraph</p>
    </main>
    
    <footer>
        <p>Footer content</p>
    </footer>
</body>

</html>
```

#### HTML Element Categories

**Block Elements:** Take full width, create line breaks
- `<div>`, `<p>`, `<h1>-<h6>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<footer>`, `<section>`, `<article>`

**Inline Elements:** Take only needed width, no line breaks
- `<span>`, `<strong>`, `<em>`, `<a>`, `<img>`, `<button>`, `<label>`

**Inline-Block:** Combination of both
- `<button>`, `<input>`, `<textarea>`, `<select>`

#### Common HTML5 Tags

```html
<!-- Headings (only use one <h1> per page) -->
<h1>Main Title</h1>
<h2>Section Heading</h2>
<h3>Subsection</h3>

<!-- Text formatting -->
<p>Paragraph of text</p>
<strong>Important text</strong>
<em>Emphasized text</em>
<mark>Highlighted text</mark>

<!-- Line breaks -->
<br>  <!-- Line break -->
<hr>  <!-- Horizontal rule -->

<!-- Grouping content -->
<div>Block container</div>
<span>Inline container</span>
```

### {{< fa code >}} Interactive Code Demo

Create a simple HTML page:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>My First Web Page</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Welcome to Web Technology</h1>
    <p>This is my first HTML page!</p>
    <p>HTML is <strong>fundamental</strong> to web development.</p>
</body>
</html>
```

**Try This:**
1. Create a file named `index.html`
2. Copy the code above
3. Open in your browser
4. You should see formatted text!

---

## {{< fa book >}} Required Readings

- MDN Web Docs: [How the Web Works](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/Getting_started_with_the_web/How_the_Web_works)
- MDN Web Docs: [What is a Web Server?](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/Common_questions/What_is_a_web_server)
- MDN Web Docs: [HTML Basics](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/Getting_started_with_the_web/HTML_basics)
- W3Schools: [HTML Tutorial](https://www.w3schools.com/html/)

## {{< fa film >}} Supplementary Videos

- YouTube: "How the Internet Works in 5 Minutes" (TED)
- YouTube: "HTTP/HTTPS Explained" (Computerphile)
- YouTube: "HTML in 100 Seconds" (Fireship)

---

## {{< fa flask >}} Practical Lab Exercise

**Lab 1.1: Create a Professional Profile Page**

**Objective:** Build a multi-section HTML page with proper structure

**Requirements:**
1. Valid HTML5 document with proper head/body
2. Header section with navigation
3. About me section with biography
4. Skills list (unordered)
5. Experience section (description list)
6. Footer with copyright
7. Semantic elements (not just divs)

**Starter Code:**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>My Professional Profile</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Add your content here -->
</body>
</html>
```

**Submission:** Save as `.html` file and test in browser

---

## {{< fa lightbulb >}} Best Practices

✅ **Do:**
- Always declare `<!DOCTYPE html>`
- Use semantic elements (`<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<footer>`)
- Use `lang` attribute on `<html>` tag
- Include meta tags for charset and viewport
- Write meaningful heading hierarchy
- Use meaningful element names

❌ **Don't:**
- Use obsolete tags (`<center>`, `<font>`)
- Skip the `DOCTYPE`
- Use `<div>` for everything
- Ignore accessibility
- Write invalid HTML

---

## Summary

| Topic | Key Takeaway |
|-------|--------------|
| Web History | Web evolved from ARPANET (1969) to modern interactive platforms |
| HTTP Protocol | Stateless request-response protocol powering the web |
| HTML Fundamentals | Markup language providing semantic structure to content |
| Document Structure | Proper organization with head, body, and semantic elements |

**Next Unit:** We'll explore advanced HTML elements (tables, forms) and start styling with CSS!

- HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
- HTTP status codes
- HTTP headers
- SSL/TLS and HTTPS

### 4. Web Browsers

- Browser rendering engines
- Browser compatibility issues
- Developer tools overview
- Progressive enhancement

### 5. Web Servers

- Apache, Nginx, IIS
- Static vs dynamic content
- Server-side processing
- Web hosting basics

## Key Concepts

::: {.callout-important}
### Important Terms

- **URL (Uniform Resource Locator)**: The address used to access resources on the web
- **HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol)**: The protocol for transferring data on the web
- **DNS (Domain Name System)**: Translates domain names to IP addresses
- **Client**: The device/application requesting resources (e.g., web browser)
- **Server**: The system that stores and delivers web resources
- **IP Address**: Unique numerical identifier for devices on a network
:::

## Code Examples

### Understanding URL Structure

```
https://www.example.com:443/path/page.html?query=value#section

Protocol: https://
Domain: www.example.com
Port: :443
Path: /path/page.html
Query: ?query=value
Fragment: #section
```

### Basic HTTP Request/Response

```http
# HTTP Request
GET /index.html HTTP/1.1
Host: www.example.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0

# HTTP Response
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html
Content-Length: 1234

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
...
</html>
```

## Practical Exercises

### Exercise 1: Browser Developer Tools

1. Open any website in Chrome or Firefox
2. Open Developer Tools (F12 or Right-click → Inspect)
3. Explore the following tabs:
   - **Elements**: View HTML structure and CSS
   - **Console**: Run JavaScript commands
   - **Network**: See all HTTP requests
   - **Application**: View cookies and storage

### Exercise 2: Analyzing HTTP Traffic

1. Open Developer Tools → Network tab
2. Visit a website and refresh the page
3. Observe:
   - Request and response headers
   - Status codes
   - File types and sizes
   - Loading timeline

## Lab Work

**[Lab 1: Setting up Development Environment](/labs/lab-01.qmd)**

In this week's lab, you will:
- Install Visual Studio Code
- Install useful extensions for web development
- Set up a project folder structure
- Create your first HTML file
- Use browser developer tools

## Additional Resources

### Videos

- [How the Internet Works in 5 Minutes](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7_LPdttKXPc)
- [HTTP Crash Course & Exploration](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iYM2zFP3Zn0)

### Tools

- [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) - Recommended code editor
- [Chrome DevTools](https://developers.google.com/web/tools/chrome-devtools) - Documentation
- [Firefox Developer Tools](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Tools) - Documentation

### Reference

- [MDN Web Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/) - Comprehensive web development documentation
- [Can I Use](https://caniuse.com/) - Browser compatibility tables
- [HTTP Status Codes](https://httpstatuses.com/) - Complete list with explanations

## What's Next?

Next week, we'll dive into HTML5 and start building web pages. Make sure you have your development environment set up and ready to go!

::: {.callout-tip}
### Preparation for Next Week

- Complete Lab 1
- Install all required software
- Create a GitHub account if you don't have one
- Review basic HTML syntax
:::
